note:
事实上，我们已经简化了所有的命名：Element也是New Vector(Riot背后的公司)的名称，而我们的旗舰矩阵托管服务Modular已经变成了Element Matrix Services。
更重要的是，RiotX现在已经退出测试版-我们的下一代Android Matrix客户端已经起飞，并作为Element展开了翅膀-最终取代了旧的Riot Android应用程序！
这个名字的改变已经酝酿了很长时间。正如我们几周前宣布更名时解释的那样，我们与某家大型游戏公司发生了重大问题，该公司阻止了我们注册Riot甚至Riot.im-当涉及到保护用户免受应用程序滥用分支的影响时，这是一个巨大的问题。其次，人们错误地认为暴乱指的是暴力，而不是我们心目中更具建设性的混乱形式。最后，在矩阵的早期，有不同的品牌来说明Riot、Modular和New Vector在生态系统…中的不同角色是有意义的。但是现在有很多Matrix客户、Matrix主机提供商和建立在Matrix之上的公司--所以所有New Vector的不同名称都会引起混淆。
我们想要一个名字，反映出我们在设计RiotX时的目标是简单和清晰；这个名字突出了我们一心一意的使命，让Element成为可以想象到的最优雅和最可用的主流通讯应用程序。Riot有一段相当曲折的历史，它一生中的大部分时间都在测试版中度过，断断续续地关注设计，直到我们去年达到1.0-但现在我们有一个专门的全职设计团队致力于Element，他们不仅设计图形，还设计应用程序中新功能的整体行为。但这并不是一项容易的任务，因为事实证明，端到端加密中最困难的部分几乎完全是UX挑战。更重要的是，呈现一个像集中式通信孤岛一样简单的全球分散通信网络令人惊讶地棘手。然而：我们已经取得了巨大的进步，以至于我们几乎认不出是过去的骚乱。元素反映了一个全新的开始，它将一味地专注于未来的主流可用性。
我们还想要一个更好地唤起数据所有权和自我主权概念的名字。元素是系统中最小的不可分割的东西-但它可以独立存在。“你可以定制它，控制它，让它成为你自己的-你可以真的置身于你的元素中！
此外，我们希望为对等矩阵提供一个面向未来的名称。*正如Matrix.org上个月宣布的那样，P2P矩阵正在大力开发-我们可以看到，在这个世界里，Element实际上将成为Matrix的一个元素，在应用程序中运行您自己的家庭服务器，这样如果您没有服务器…，您就可以进行通信。甚至是互联网。事实上，我们几天前在TestFlight上发布了第一个针对iOS的P2P Riot实验性版本--所以我们可以清楚地看到我们将在P2P元素中的未来！
最后，我们认为它是一个很酷的名字：D，它与术语Matrix非常吻合，同时又不像Vector那样书呆子。我们显然意识到Element(再次)既是一个词典单词，也是一个数学术语-但在实践中，现在查看Element的搜索结果，排名最靠前的是词典网站(！)。而且这个领域是敞开的。相反，在VPN上的处女浏览器中，Riot是谷歌在Riot上的第四大热门；仅次于某家游戏公司。“换句话说，我们已经证明，我们可以成功地采用字典中的单词-如果你确实发现自己在数学迷宫中迷失了方向，只需加上”聊天“这个词就可以回到正轨。(对于所有在这一点上怒气冲冲的线性代数学家：我们承诺会通过最终解决Element中的LaTeX支持来弥补您的损失！)。
这一变化将不可避免地具有破坏性，我们痛苦地意识到，我们花了四年时间建立Riot的声誉，并说服每个人将他们的朋友、家人和团队转移到它上面。*重申我们在宣布这一变化时所说的话，我们知道许多阅读这篇文章的人都会冒着风险让人们采用Riot，我们真的很理解不得不向你的用户解释这一变化是多么令人沮丧。然而，毫无疑问，现在是脱下我们的皮做出改变的正确时机，我们希望人们会成长为比Riot更热爱元素。为了帮助过渡，我们暂时将可安装的应用程序命名为“Element(Riot.im)”，以帮助人们重新定位(例如，当按名称搜索Riot时)。
最后但并非最不重要的一点是：Element不仅仅是一个新名称--它在Android、iOS和Web上都有一系列巨大的改进。
我们将在接下来的几天里完整地报道这些内容，但最大的头条新闻是RiotX(我们对Riot Android的彻底重写)已经退出测试版，并取代Riot Android成为元素-完全支持VoIP通话和Widget！Riot Android用户将神奇地自动升级到Element；旧的RiotX应用程序将在适当的时候停用。FDroid可能会将Element作为一款全新的应用程序发布。与此同时，在iOS上，我们现在完全支持iOS 13，还提供了全新的推送通知支持(这要归功于苹果噩梦般的反对PushKit)。
在Element Web上，我们对UI进行了大规模刷新，漂亮的新字体(Inter)大大提高了易读性，我们还完全重写了Room List控件-添加了房间预览(！！)、字母排序、可调整大小的列表、改进的通知UI等等。我们还完成了进一步改进端到端加密设置流程的第一阶段-让用户生成恢复密钥，而不是在第一次设置E2EE时强迫他们选择恢复密码(下一阶段是推迟设置E2EE，直到用户真正开始在加密房间中交谈)。
所以你找到了。欢迎来到Riot的一个全新的开始：欢迎来到您的新元素，主流Matrix用户也将在这里尽情享受-这将为通过Matrix在世界各地更广泛地采用开放、安全、分散的通信铺平道路。
另外，请查看我们全新的网站，了解更多关于我们美丽新世界的信息！
note_en:
In fact we have simplified all our naming: Element is also the name for New Vector (the company behind Riot) while Modular, our flagship Matrix hosting service, has become Element Matrix Services.
  What’s more,  RiotX is now out of beta - our next generation Matrix client for Android has flown the nest and spread its wings as Element - replacing the old Riot Android app at last!
 This name change has been a long time in the making. As we explained when we announced the rebrand  a few weeks ago, we’ve had major issues with a certain gigantic games company which has blocked us from being able to trademark Riot or even Riot.im - a huge issue when it comes to defending users against abusive forks of the app. Secondly, people incorrectly assume Riot refers to violence, rather than the more constructive forms of chaos we had in mind. Lastly, it made sense in the early days of Matrix to have different brands to illustrate the different roles of Riot, Modular and New Vector in the ecosystem… but nowadays there are loads of Matrix clients, Matrix hosting providers and companies building on Matrix - and so all New Vector’s different names were just causing confusion.
  We want a name that reflects the emphasis on simplicity and clarity that we aimed for when designing RiotX; a name that highlights our single-minded mission to make Element the most elegant and usable mainstream comms app imaginable.  Riot had a pretty chequered history, spending much of its life in beta, with intermittent attention to design until we hit 1.0 last year - but nowadays we have a dedicated full-time design team working on Element, who not only design the graphics but the overall behaviour of new features in the app.  This is not an easy task, given it turns out the hardest bits of end-to-end encryption are almost entirely UX challenges.  What’s more, presenting a global decentralised communication network as simply as a centralised communication island is surprisingly tricky.  However: we have been making massive progress, to the extent that we are barely recognisable as the Riot of the past. Element reflects a clean start to focus monomaniacally on mainstream usability in future.
 We also want a name that better evokes the idea of data ownership and self-sovereignty.  An element is the smallest indivisible thing in a system - yet one which can stand alone.  You can customise it, control it and make it your own - you can literally be in your Element!
 Moreover, we want a name that will be future-proof for peer-to-peer Matrix.  As Matrix.org announced last month,  P2P Matrix is in heavy development - and we can see a world where Element will literally be an element of Matrix, running your own homeserver within the app, so you can communicate if you don’t have a server… or even Internet. In fact, we published the first experimental build of  P2P Riot for iOS on Testflight a few days ago - so we can clearly see a future where we will be in our P2P element!
 Finally, we think it’s a cool name :D  It fits in nicely with the term Matrix while not being as nerdy as Vector.  We’re obviously aware that Element is (once again) both a dictionary word and a mathematical term - but in practice, looking at search results for Element right now, the top hits are for dictionary sites(!) and the field is wide open.  Conversely, in a virgin browser on VPN, Riot is the 4th hit on Google for Riot; second only to a certain games company.  In other words, we’ve shown that we can successfully adopt dictionary words - and if you do find yourself lost searching in a maze of mathematics, just throw in the word ‘chat’ to get back on track.  (For all the linear algebraists who are spitting with rage at this point: we promise we’ll make it up to you by finally sorting out  latex support in Element!)
 This change is inevitably going to be disruptive, and we’re painfully aware that we’ve spent four years building up Riot’s reputation and persuading everyone to move their friends, families and teams onto it.  To reiterate what we said when we announced the change, we know that many people reading this will have put their necks on the line to get folks to adopt Riot, and we really appreciate how frustrating it may be to have to explain the change to your users.  However, this is unquestionably the right time to shed our skin and make the change, and we hope folks will grow to love Element much more than they ever did Riot.  To aid the transition, we’ve named the installable apps “Element (Riot.im)” for a while to help people get reoriented (e.g. when searching by name for Riot).
 Last but not least: Element isn’t just a new name - it comes with a massive suite of improvements across Android, iOS and Web.
 We’ll write about these in full over the coming days, but the big headlines are that RiotX (our ground-up rewrite of Riot Android) has exited beta, and replaces Riot Android as Element - complete with VoIP calls and Widget support! Riot Android users will magically autoupgrade into Element; the old RiotX app will be retired in due course. FDroid is likely releasing Element as an entirely new app.  Meanwhile On iOS we now have full support for iOS 13, complete with entirely new push notification support (thanks to Apple’s nightmarish  deprecation of PushKit).
  On Element Web, we’ve given the UI a massive refresh, with a beautiful new font ( Inter) giving much improved legibility, and we’ve  completely rewritten the Room List control - adding in room previews(!!), alphabetic ordering, resizable lists, improved notification UI and more. We’ve also landed the first phase of further improving the end-to-end encryption setup process - letting users generate a recovery key rather than forcing them to select a recovery passphrase when setting up E2EE for the first time (the next phase is to delay setting up E2EE until the user actually starts talking in encrypted rooms).
  So there you have it. Welcome to a whole new beginning for Riot: welcome to your new Element, one where mainstream Matrix users will enjoy themselves too - and which will pave the way for wider adoption of open, secure, decentralised communication via Matrix throughout the world.
  P.S. Check out our  all-new website for much more information about our brave new world!
title: 骚乱现在是要素

note:
今天，Graphcore宣布了一款新的芯片GC200，以及在其上运行的新的IPU机器M2000，Graphcore表示，这是第一台在“披萨盒大小”下实现Petaflop处理能力的人工智能计算机。
Graphcore表示，GC200没有单独销售的计划，只会在M2000上市。该公司首席执行官兼联合创始人奈杰尔·图恩(Nigel Toon)表示，M2000目前正在向早期接入客户发货，并将在今年年底前更广泛地面向金融服务、医疗保健、技术等领域的客户，“无论在哪里使用人工智能”。
Toon指出，这是Graphcore即将发布的第二代硬件，也是近两年来的第一代。
IPU机器使用7 nm GC200 IPU芯片中的4个，每个芯片上都有594亿个晶体管。Graphcore表示，潜在地，多达64,000个IPU可以连接在一起，以创建高达16 exaflops的计算能力和PB内存的巨大并行处理器，以支持具有数万亿参数的模型。我们的想法是，这些项目可以在必要时扩大规模。
这些举措正值Graphcore和人工智能硬件行业的关键时刻。这家英国初创企业在英伟达(Nvidia)和英特尔-Graphcore(Intel-Graphcore)等处理器领域与利维坦展开竞争。今年5月，这家英国初创企业以近20亿美元的估值进一步筹集了1.5亿美元，以与它们竞争。Toon表示，目前为止筹集的4.5亿美元已经足够了，像微软和其他客户已经在其账面上-但也有太多其他公司在制造人工智能芯片。直到5月份，NVIDIA才推出了自己的最新芯片A100，这是它第一个基于安培的GPU，承诺性能为5千万亿次浮点。
Graphcore及其领导人Toon-与他的联合创始人西蒙·诺尔斯(Simon Knowles)曾将之前一家名为Icera的初创公司出售给NVIDIA-辩称，其IPU方法比NVIDIA正在采取的GPU路线更高效、更先进。
他说：“我们正在努力打造易于安装到您现有计算基础设施中的产品。”“这意味着你最多可以扩展到数千个议会联盟处理器。”他补充说，这意味着各国议会联盟的拥有成本可以降低10-20倍，这反过来又意味着硬件的接受速度更快。
Toon表示，虽然其他芯片制造商继续在开发与人工智能并行的其他一些处理应用程序-例如移动设备或量子芯片-但Graphcore仍坚定地专注于人工智能应用程序，他表示，这仍然是“我们发展业务和增加更多客户的巨大机会”。
“我们100%专注于用于人工智能的硅处理器，并致力于建立可以插入现有中心的系统。如果CPU或GPU已经运行良好，我们为什么还要构建它们呢？这只是一个不同的工具箱。“。他说，他认为量子或分子计算的出现将需要10-15年的时间，这一轨迹可能会给试图在这一领域与IBM等大公司竞争的较小初创公司带来许多新的挑战。
图恩指出，人工智能处于新冠肺炎大流行加速的趋势中-不仅围绕健康危机和抗击病毒本身正在追求的许多应用程序，而且还围绕着工作和改善由此产生的其他服务的流程。
图恩指出，“我们在技术和人才方面的投资可能会再烧掉1亿美元”--该公司目前有450名员工，“但我们的收入也在不断增长，我们今天拥有的3亿美元现金应该足以让我们实现快速盈利。”
note_en:
Today, it is announcing a new chip, the GC200, and a new IPU Machine that runs on it, the M2000, which Graphcore says is the first AI computer to achieve a petaflop of processing power “in the size of a pizza box.”
 Graphcore says that there are no plans for the GC200 to be sold separately, and it will come only in the M2000. Nigel Toon, the CEO and co-founder, said the M2000 is now shipping to early access customers and will be more widely by the end of this year to customers in applications in areas like financial services, healthcare, technology and more, “wherever AI is used.”
  This is the second generation of Graphcore’s hardware to be released, and its first in just under two years, Toon notes.
 The IPU Machine uses four of the 7nm GC200 IPU chips, with the GC200 featuring 59.4 billion transistors on each chip. Potentially, Graphcore says that up to 64,000 IPUs can be connected together to create a vast parallel processor of up to 16 exaflops of computing power and petabytes of memory to support models with trillions of parameters. The idea is that these can be scaled up as necessary.
 The moves come at a key moment both for Graphcore and the AI hardware industry. The UK upstart  competes against leviathans in the world of processors like Nvidia and Intel — Graphcore raised a  further $150 million in May at a nearly $2 billion valuation to compete against them, and Toon says the $450 million it’s raised so far is enough for now, with customers like Microsoft and others already on its books — but also a plethora of other companies building AI chips. And it was only in May that Nvidia unveiled its own latest chip, the A100, its first Ampere-based GPU that promises 5 petaflops of performance.
 Graphcore and its leader Toon — who, with his co-founder Simon Knowles, had  sold a previous startup called Icera to Nvidia — argue that its IPU approach is more efficient and advanced than the GPU route that Nvidia is taking.
 “We are trying to build products that are easy to put into your existing compute infrastructure,” he said. “It means you can scale up to thousands of IPU processors.” And, he added, that means that the cost of ownership can be 10-20 times lower for the IPU approach, which in turn translates to faster take-up of the hardware.
 Toon says that while other chipmakers continue to work on a number of other processing applications in parallel with AI — for example for mobile devices, or quantum chips — Graphcore is remaining firmly focused on AI applications, which he says remains a “massive opportunity for us to grow our business and add more customers.”
 “ We’re 100% focused on silicon processors for AI, and on building systems that can plug into existing centers. Why would we want to build CPUs or GPUs if those already work well? This is just a different toolbox.” He said he believes it will be a 10-15 year window before quantum or molecular computing to come along , a trajectory that might  pose a lot  of challenges  for smaller startups trying to build in that area against biggies like IBM.
 Toon noted that AI stands among the trends that the COVID-19 pandemic has accelerated — not just  around the many applications being pursued around the health crisis and fighting the virus itself, but also around working and improving processes for other services resulting from that.
 “We’ll probably burn $100 million more investing in technology and people” — the company now has 450 employees, Toon noted, “but our revenues are also ramping, and the $300 million in cash we have today should be sufficient to get us to a a fast and profitable business.”
title: Graphcore推出新的G200芯片和在其上运行的可扩展M2000 IPU机器
title_en: Graphcore unveils new G200 chip and the expandable M2000 IPU Machine that runs on them

note:
一段时间以来，我一直打算让我的网站更具可持续性，但我一如既往地屈服于人的懒惰特性。但是今天早上读了格里·麦戈文关于网络浪费的帖子后，我想我已经拖得够久了。
所以我进行了一次网页性能测试，得到了一些糟糕的结果：我的网站在使用3G数据网络的Moto G4上加载需要一分钟以上的时间。在诺丁汉的台式电脑上使用1.5 Mbps的DSL也同样糟糕。我的网站充斥着大图片和一堆JavaScript，这意味着它正在消耗大量的能量来传输这些比特和字节。
但是有多少能量呢？我用“碳计算器”网站找出了答案。事实证明，
啊哈。那真讨厌。我的网站每上线一年，光是首页我就应该种下4棵树。但是，相反，我的懒惰让大气中充满了越来越多的碳。
我必须做点什么，这件事已经持续得够久了，所以我承诺采取一些行动。
我会把我的网站移到一个使用可再生能源的虚拟主机提供商那里，绿色网络基金会的目录上列出了这一点。
我会摆脱高耗能的网站主题，直到我可以引入一个轻量级和易访问的主题。干完。
为了表示我致力于成为一个好的网民，我将在所有页面添加网站Carbon徽章(Done)和绿色网络基金会的可更新主机徽章。一旦我改进了一些东西，我就会添加cion.txt。
总有一天，我会着手建造一个太阳能电池组，并通过我的家庭连接来运行我的网站，但在那之前，我会采取一些小步骤来移除、最小化和清理我在网络上的存在。
note_en:
For a while I’ve been intending to make my website more sustainable, but I succumbed, as I often do, to the human trait of sloth. But this morning after reading  Gerry McGovern’s post on webwaste, I thought I’d procrastinated long enough.
 So I ran a web page performance test and got some  grim results: my website takes over a minute to load on a Moto G4 on using 3G data networks. It’s just as bad  using a desktop PC in Nottingham on 1.5Mbps DSL. My website is bloated with large images and a bunch of JavaScript, which means it’s eating up lots of energy transmitting those bits and bytes.
 But how much energy? I used the  Website Carbon Calculator to find out. Turns out that
  Eugh. That’s disgusting. For each year my website has been online, I should have planted 4 trees just for the homepage alone. But, instead, my laziness has filled the atmosphere with more and more carbon.
 I have to do something, this has gone on long enough, so I’m committing to some actions.
 I’ll move my site to a web hosting provider using renewable energy, one that’s listed on the  Green Web Foundation’s directory.
    I’ll get rid of  my energy-guzzling site theme until I can introduce one that’s lightweight and accessible.  DONE
 To show my committment to being a good web citizen, I’ll add the  Website Carbon Badge to all pages ( DONE) and the  Green Web Foundation’s renewable hosting badge. Once I’ve improved things, I’ll add  carbon.txt.
 One day I’ll actually get around to building a solar-powered battery bank and run my site off my home connection, but until then I’m taking small steps to remove, minimise and clean-up my presence on the web.
title: 这个网站正在扼杀地球
title_en: This Website Is Killing the Planet

note:
C系列融资由Draper ESPRIT牵头，现有投资者Amadeus Capital Partners、Blackfin Tech和Psience Capital参与其中。Rvelin在2018年9月披露了1000万美元的B系列资金。
Ravvelin于2016年推出，利用机器学习和图形网络技术帮助在线企业减少欺诈损失，提高订单接受率。这个想法是要废除更粗糙的、基于规则的系统，并使用机器学习来否定假阳性，并给商家更多接受客户/交易的信心。
至于产品市场适合性，ravelin表示，它最初在大规模的食品和出租车市场取得了成功，但后来扩展到旅游、票务、娱乐、游戏、博彩和零售领域。除了识别信用卡欺诈外，ravelin还与客户合作，查找被泄露的账户(称为“账户接管”)，发现激励滥用行为，并打击市场中的供应商欺诈行为。
账户接管是指欺诈者使用在数据泄露中暴露的凭证来接管个人的在线账户供自己使用或在黑暗网络上销售。Ravvelin的首席营销官Gerry Carr解释说：“我们有一种产品，首先可以帮助保护账户，识别风险账户，并帮助商家收回原始用户的账户。”“这是一个复杂的问题，而且由于容易做到，这是一个快速增长的问题。”
奖励滥用是指用户重复使用和/或共享优惠券和注册奖励来欺骗商家。为了防止这种情况，ravelin能够绘制出用户网络及其相关的代金券代码，以发现某个代金券是否已被其他用户使用并阻止它。
供应商欺诈通常会影响客户、快递公司或产品供应商欺骗市场的市场。卡尔说：“有很多种方式可以做到这一点，一个简单的例子就是供应商使用假账户以现金交付的方式下订单。在这种情况下，市场会提前向餐厅付款。一旦他们收到付款，餐厅就会取消订单，并保留付款。我们可以帮助市场识别网络中的异常活动，制止欺诈行为“。
ravelin还在开发“ravelin Accept”，这是一款旨在帮助企业浏览PDS2并自信地接受而不是拒绝更多交易的产品。PSD2意味着交易将需要更多身份验证。
卡尔解释说：“这冒着很多交易失败的风险，因为消费者正在为加强身份验证而苦苦挣扎，商家也不确定如何获得安全身份验证的豁免。”Ravelin Accept将内置有关主要发行人喜欢如何管理交易的情报。它将在可能的情况下将交易路由到豁免以进行身份验证，如果不是，[它]将动态管理该步骤，以使其获得最好的接受机会。今年年底PSD2的硬期限应该会看到对ravelin Accept的巨大需求，以帮助提高录取率。
与此同时，ravelin表示，它将利用C系列融资进一步投资于这些创新，并在全球范围内接触到更多的市场和行业。
Draper ESPRIT的Vinoth Jayakumar评论说：“我们的模式是长期投资于创新。ravelin与这一论点完全一致。Ravelin的团队是世界级的，并继续努力推动他们的产品…的边界。真正让我们兴奋的是他们为客户解决的一系列问题，以及他们正在开发的一套产品。“
note_en:
The Series C round is led by Draper Esprit, with participation from existing investors Amadeus Capital Partners, BlackFin Tech, and Passion Capital.  Ravelin    disclosed $10 million in Series B funding in September 2018.
 Launched in 2016, Ravelin utilises machine learning and graph network technologies to help online businesses reduce losses to fraud and improve acceptance rates of orders. The idea is to do away with cruder, rule-based systems and use machine learning to negate false positives and give merchants more confidence accepting customers/transactions.
  With regards to product-market fit, Ravelin says it first found success with large-scale food and cab-ride marketplaces, but has since expanded into travel, ticketing, entertainment, gaming, gambling, and retail. In addition to identifying card fraud, Ravelin also works with clients to find compromised accounts (referred to as “account takeover”), spot incentive abuse and tackle supplier fraud in marketplaces.
 Account takeover is where fraudsters use credentials that have been exposed in data breaches to take over an individual’s online account for their own use or to sell on the dark web. “We have a product that helps secure accounts in the first place, identify at risk accounts and help the merchant reclaim the account for the original user,” explains Ravelin’s chief marketing officerGerry Carr. “It’s a complex problem and due to the ease that it can be done, a fast growing issue”.
 Incentive abuse sees users re-using and/or sharing vouchers and sign-up incentives to defraud a merchant. To prevent this, Ravelin is able to map out the network of users and their associated voucher codes to spot if a voucher is already used by another user and block it.
 Supplier fraud typically affects marketplaces where the customer, the courier or the product supplier is working to defraud the marketplace. “There are many ways this can happen,” says Carr, “a simple example might be a supplier uses a fake account to place an order with cash delivery. In this scenario the marketplace advances the restaurant the payment. Once they receive the payment, the restaurant cancels the order and keeps the payment. We can help a marketplace identify anomalous activity in the network and put a stop to fraudulent behaviour”.
 Ravelin is also developing “Ravelin Accept,” a product aimed at helping businesses navigate PDS2 and confidently accept rather than reject more transactions. PSD2 means there will be a lot more authentication required for transactions.
 “This risks a lot of failed transactions as consumers struggle with the step-up authentication and merchants are unsure about how to get exemptions to the secure authentication,” explains Carr. “Ravelin Accept will have built-in intelligence about how the major issuers like to manage transactions. It will route a transaction to an exemption to authentication where possible, and where it is not, [it] will manage that step-up dynamically to give it the best chance of acceptance. The hard deadline of PSD2 at the end of this year should see significant demand for Ravelin Accept to help with acceptance rates”.
 Meanwhile, Ravelin says it will use its Series C round to further invest in these innovations, and to reach more markets and industries globally.
 Comments Draper Esprit’s Vinoth Jayakumar: “Our model is to invest in innovation over the long term. Ravelin perfectly aligns with that thesis. The team at Ravelin are world-class and continue to work to push the boundaries of their products… What got us really excited was the range of problems they solve for clients and the suite of products they are developing”.
title: 欺诈检测初创公司RAVELIN获得2000万美元的C系列投资
title_en: Fraud detection startup Ravelin secures $20M Series C
